fault_hypothesis: On line 59, `idx = int(len(values) * q)` computes an index equal to `len(values)` when `q=1.0`, causing an out-of-bounds IndexError on line 60. The index should be clamped to `len(values) - 1`.

constraints.intent: Ensure the computed index is strictly less than the length of values to avoid IndexError.
After computing idx, verify it is a valid index into values.
Before using idx to index into values, confirm it does not exceed the last valid index.
The returned value must be an element that actually exists in the input sequence.
